original_description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute. Supports two modes: Subagent Mode (fresh subagent per task with two-phase review, for platforms with subagent support like Claude Code) and Sequential Mode (single-agent execution with review checkpoints). Do NOT trigger for: parallel dispatch of independent investigations without review stages (use dispatching-parallel-agents); orchestrating 3+ tasks across multiple sessions (use strategic-looping with executing-plans); creating or writing implementation plans (use writing-plans); creating or modifying skills (use skill-creator).

train_results.query: I've got a plan.md from our design session yesterday, let's start executing it
we're on step 3 of the plan, the first two checkpoints passed, continue from where we left off
the design doc at docs/architecture/redesign.md has all the tasks laid out — execute it in subagent mode
we brainstormed this yesterday and I saved the output to brainstorm_output.md. now execute it
run through the implementation plan I pasted above, use two-phase review between tasks
there are 4 completely independent investigation tasks here — search the codebase for each in parallel
ok the plan is written out in PLAN.md, can you start working through the tasks
we're mid-plan but I realize the approach is wrong — let me brainstorm an alternative before continuing
what does the existing auth code actually do before I start changing it? write characterization tests first
I need to write an implementation plan for the new billing system before we start coding
checkpoint 2 failed on the plan — write a postmortem and figure out why before we continue
help me structure a technical spec for the cache layer redesign so we can hand it off to the team

test_results.query: platform doesn't support subagents here so just do sequential mode on this plan please
here's the plan we agreed on: 1) refactor auth middleware 2) add rate limiting 3) update tests. go ahead and implement it
my HANDOFF has a full implementation plan in it — pick it up and run the tasks sequentially
the plan has 5 tasks, waves 1-2 are independent but wave 3 depends on both — start working through it
haven't thought through the design yet, let's brainstorm the auth refactor before touching any code
these 6 tasks are all independent with no shared state, dispatch them all at once
we have a 3-month roadmap across multiple sessions, need to orchestrate it with checkpoints and memory between runs
can you create a step-by-step plan for migrating our postgres schema to the new format
